title: Adana Kebab

seo.title: Authentic Adana Kebab Recipe: Make Turkish Skewers at Home
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Adana Kebab! Our easy-to-follow recipe brings the fiery flavors of Turkish Adana to your kitchen. Perfect for a delicious lunch or dinner.
seo.h1: Authentic Adana Kebab Recipe: A Taste of Turkey at Home
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Adana Kebab
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Adana Kebab
Pro Tips for the Best Adana Kebab Results
Serving Suggestions for Your Adana Kebab
Variations and Substitutions for Adana Kebab
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) About Adana Kebab
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Adana Kebab skewers served with grilled vegetables and flatbread on a rustic wooden board.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, spicy flavors of homemade Adana Kebab, perfectly grilled and ready to enjoy!

description: Adana Kebab is a renowned Turkish dish featuring hand-minced meat, typically lamb or a mix of lamb and beef, seasoned with red pepper flakes and grilled on wide skewers. Its distinctive spicy flavor and succulent texture make it a beloved staple of Turkish cuisine, perfect for a hearty lunch. Enjoy the rich, smoky taste of this traditional kebab, often served with fresh vegetables and flatbread.

ingredients.item: Ground lamb or a mix of lamb and beef

instructions.title: Prepare the Vegetables
instructions.text: Finely mince the red bell pepper, green bell pepper, and red onion. You can use a food processor, but be careful not to create a paste; aim for a very fine dice. Squeeze out any excess liquid from the minced vegetables to prevent the kebab mixture from becoming too wet. Mince the garlic cloves.

instructions.title: Mix the Kebab Meat
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb (or meat mix) with the finely minced and drained vegetables, minced garlic, hot red pepper flakes, sweet red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper. Use your hands to thoroughly knead the mixture for at least 5-7 minutes. This is crucial for developing the meat's texture and ensuring it sticks to the skewers.

instructions.title: Chill the Mixture
instructions.text: Once the meat mixture is well-kneaded and cohesive, cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes. This chilling period helps the flavors meld and makes the meat easier to handle and shape onto the skewers.

instructions.title: Shape the Kebabs
instructions.text: Preheat your grill to medium-high heat. Take a portion of the chilled meat mixture, about 100-120g, and firmly press it around a wide, flat metal skewer. Use your hands to evenly distribute the meat along the skewer, forming a long, flat, oval shape about 1 cm thick. Ensure the meat adheres well to the skewer to prevent it from falling off during grilling.

instructions.title: Grill the Adana Kebabs
instructions.text: Place the shaped kebabs directly on the hot grill. Grill for 4-6 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until they are nicely browned, slightly charred, and cooked through. The cooking time may vary depending on the thickness of the kebabs and the heat of your grill. Be careful not to overcook them to keep them juicy.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Once cooked, carefully remove the Adana Kebabs from the grill. Serve them hot, typically with lavash or pita bread, sliced raw onions with sumac and parsley, grilled tomatoes and peppers, and a fresh salad like gavurdagi salatasi. A squeeze of lemon juice often complements the rich flavors.

tips: For authentic texture, try to hand-mince the meat if you have a very sharp knife, or ask your butcher for twice-ground lamb with about 20-25% fat.
The key to a good Adana Kebab is proper kneading of the meat mixture. This process binds the proteins, making the kebab stick to the skewer and giving it a unique chewy yet tender texture.
Serve Adana Kebab with plenty of fresh herbs like parsley and mint, a side of creamy cacik (cucumber yogurt dip), and a sprinkle of sumac on your onion salad for an authentic Turkish experience.
article: Adana Kebab is a beloved Turkish Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
